item.title:
IRAN TARGETS GULF ENERGY FACILITIES, SAYS TASMIN
Iran’s Tasnim news agency lists Gulf energy sites as “legitimate targets,” warning they could be struck soon. Facilities named:
🔸Qatar: Ras Laffan refinery phases 1 & 2, Mesaieed Petrochemical
🔸Saudi Arabia: Samref refinery, Jubail petrochemical complex
🔸UAE: Al Hosn gas field
item.description:
IRAN TARGETS GULF ENERGY FACILITIES, SAYS TASMIN

Iran’s Tasnim news agency lists Gulf energy sites as “legitimate targets,” warning they could be struck soon.
Facilities named:

🔸Qatar: Ras Laffan refinery phases 1 & 2, Mesaieed Petrochemical

🔸Saudi Arabia: Samref refinery, Jubail petrochemical complex

🔸UAE: Al Hosn gas field

item.title:
US WHOLESALE INFLATION SPIKES MORE THAN EXPECTED
US producer prices rose 0.7% in February, above forecasts, with core wholesale inflation up 0.5%. The increase reflects higher costs for goods and services, even as consumer inflation had recently eased before rising energy prices tied to the Iran war.
item.description:
US WHOLESALE INFLATION SPIKES MORE THAN EXPECTED

US producer prices rose 0.7% in February, above forecasts, with core wholesale inflation up 0.5%. The increase reflects higher costs for goods and services, even as consumer inflation had recently eased before rising energy prices tied to the Iran war.

item.title:
ISRAEL STRIKES IRAN ENERGY TARGET WITH U.S. SUPPORT
A senior Israeli official says the Israeli Air Force hit a major natural gas processing facility in southwest Iran, targeting critical energy infrastructure in coordination with and approved by the U.S. military leadership
item.description:
ISRAEL STRIKES IRAN ENERGY TARGET WITH U.S. SUPPORT

item.title: Iran’s giant South Pars gas field — the world’s largest shared with Qatar — is a key source of pipeline gas to Turkey via the Tabriz–Ankara line. Disruptions to those flows could force Ankara to seek more spot LNG on already tight global markets.
item.description:
Iran’s giant South Pars gas field — the world’s largest shared with Qatar — is a key source of pipeline gas to Turkey via the Tabriz–Ankara line. Disruptions to those flows could force Ankara to seek more spot LNG on already tight global markets.

*Walter Bloomberg (@DeItaone)

PART OF IRAN'S SOUTH PARS PETROCHEMICAL FACILITIES HAVE BEEN ATTACKED - TASNIM

— https://nitter.net/DeItaone/status/2034230579521339655#m

item.title:
AI COULD AUTOMATE 93% OF U.S. JOB TASKS
A new study finds AI could handle parts of 93% of U.S. jobs, potentially shifting $4.5 trillion in labor costs. Researchers analyzed 18,000+ tasks across 1,000 jobs, with software development, finance, management, legal, and office roles most affected. Cognizant CTO Babak Hodjat notes adoption is uneven, but rapid AI breakthroughs—like multimodal and agentic AI—are accelerating automation. Some physical and care jobs, like construction and healthcare support, see smaller but growing AI impact.
Impact doesn’t mean job loss: AI augments human work, improving efficiency and output. Globally, AI could influence $15 trillion in labor value, with its capabilities expanding fast.
item.description:
AI COULD AUTOMATE 93% OF U.S. JOB TASKS

A new study finds AI could handle parts of 93% of U.S. jobs, potentially shifting $4.5 trillion in labor costs. Researchers analyzed 18,000+ tasks across 1,000 jobs, with software development, finance, management, legal, and office roles most affected.
Cognizant CTO Babak Hodjat notes adoption is uneven, but rapid AI breakthroughs—like multimodal and agentic AI—are accelerating automation. Some physical and care jobs, like construction and healthcare support, see smaller but growing AI impact.

Impact doesn’t mean job loss: AI augments human work, improving efficiency and output. Globally, AI could influence $15 trillion in labor value, with its capabilities expanding fast.

item.title:
TRUMP: DEMOCRATS’ DHS SHUTDOWN CREATES AIRPORT CHAOS
“The Democrats’ intentional DHS shutdown is wreaking havoc at airports. Their radical demands are unreasonable. They are fully to blame and must pay in the Midterms. MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!”
item.description:
TRUMP: DEMOCRATS’ DHS SHUTDOWN CREATES AIRPORT CHAOS

“The Democrats’ intentional DHS shutdown is wreaking havoc at airports. Their radical demands are unreasonable. They are fully to blame and must pay in the Midterms. MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!”

item.title:
Pinned: IMPEACHMENT ODDS CLIMB TO 72%
Prediction market data shows the probability of Donald Trump being impeached before 2028 has risen to 72%, trending steadily higher in recent months. https://kalshi.com/markets/kximpeach/president-impeached/kximpeach?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
IMPEACHMENT ODDS CLIMB TO 72%

Prediction market data shows the probability of Donald Trump being impeached before 2028 has risen to 72%, trending steadily higher in recent months.
kalshi.com/markets/kximpeach…

item.description: SPOT GOLD FALLS OVER 1% INTRADAY TO $4,955.34/OZ

item.title:
$M - MACY’S BEATS Q4, OFFERS CAUTIOUS OUTLOOK
Macy's reported Q4 sales of $7.64B (above $7.62B est.) and adjusted EPS of $1.67 (vs. $1.53 est.), with net income at $507M and comp sales up 1.8%.
Results were boosted by a $328M pre-tax legal settlement gain.
For FY, Macy’s guides comp sales from -0.5% to +0.5% and EPS of $1.90–$2.10 (below $2.18 est.), citing tariff pressures—especially in Q1. The company is also expanding its store revamp strategy to 75 more locations as part of its 2026 plan.
item.description:
$M - MACY’S BEATS Q4, OFFERS CAUTIOUS OUTLOOK

Macy's reported Q4 sales of $7.64B (above $7.62B est.) and adjusted EPS of $1.67 (vs. $1.53 est.), with net income at $507M and comp sales up 1.8%.

Results were boosted by a $328M pre-tax legal settlement gain.

For FY, Macy’s guides comp sales from -0.5% to +0.5% and EPS of $1.90–$2.10 (below $2.18 est.), citing tariff pressures—especially in Q1. The company is also expanding its store revamp strategy to 75 more locations as part of its 2026 plan.

item.title:
TIM COOK IN CHINA AS APP STORE PRESSURE BUILDS
Tim Cook visited China for Apple Inc.’s 50th anniversary event in Chengdu, just days after the company cut its App Store commission to 25% following talks with regulators.
The move comes amid growing pressure from Beijing to loosen App Store rules, with calls to reduce fees further and allow third-party payments. Apple continues to face regulatory scrutiny over its control of in-app purchases and platform restrictions.
item.description:
TIM COOK IN CHINA AS APP STORE PRESSURE BUILDS

Tim Cook visited China for Apple Inc.’s 50th anniversary event in Chengdu, just days after the company cut its App Store commission to 25% following talks with regulators.

The move comes amid growing pressure from Beijing to loosen App Store rules, with calls to reduce fees further and allow third-party payments. Apple continues to face regulatory scrutiny over its control of in-app purchases and platform restrictions.
